items.hook: ‘People treat each other as disposable’: dating columnist turned novelist Annie Lord on love and sex in the age of apps
items.body:
Her breakup memoir and Vogue column made her the voice of modern dating. As her debut is published, she talks about single life, oversharing and why she still believes she’ll find love

items.hook: In Diamond, Missouri, the George Washington Carver National Monument becomes the first United States National Monument in honor of an African American.
items.body: Diamond is a city in north central Newton County, Missouri, United States, located southeast of Joplin. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Morgan Park Academy (MPA) is a coeducational, college preparatory, independent day school serving pre-kindergarten through 12th grade.
items.body: It is located in the Morgan Park neighborhood on the south side of Chicago, Illinois. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: The 100 best novels of all time
items.body: The top 100 novels of all time published in English, as voted for by authors, critics and academics worldwide. How many have you read?

items.hook: Storming of the Bastille in Paris. This event escalates the widespread discontent into the French Revolution. Bastille Day is still celebrated annually in France.
items.body: The Storming of the Bastille, also known as Fall of the Bastille, which occurred in Paris, France, on 14 July 1789, was an act of political violence by revolutionary insurgents who attempted to storm and seize control of the medieval armoury, fortress, and political prison known as the Bastille. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Hernán Díaz Arrieta (1891–1984), known by the pseudonym Alone, was a Chilean writer, literary and film critic, and civil servant.
items.body: Alone was awarded the National Prize for Literature in 1959. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Rear Admiral Sven Yngve Ekstrand was a Swedish Navy officer.
items.body: He was Chief of the Naval Staff from 1939 to 1942, Commander-in-Chief of the Coastal Fleet from 1942 to 1945 and the East Coast Naval District from 1945 to 1951. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Rus Flight 9633 crashes during takeoff from Chkalovsky Airport, killing all 10 people on board.
items.body: Rus Flight 9633 refers to the crash involving an IL-76TD aircraft operated by Rus Airlines from Chkalovsky Airport to Taiyuan Wusu Airport with intermediate stops at Alykel Airport and Bratsk Airport. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Ro-Pat-In Corporation (ElectRo-Patent-Instruments) was founded by Adolph Rickenbacher and George Beauchamp in 1931 to manufacture and distribute electrically amplified musical instruments.
items.body: Beauchamp designed the instruments, assisted by Paul Barth and Harry Watson from National String Instrument Corporation. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: A superhabitable world is a hypothetical type of planet or moon that is better suited than Earth for the emergence and evolution of life.
items.body: The concept was introduced in a 2014 paper by René Heller and John Armstrong, in which they criticized the language used in the search for habitable exoplanets and proposed clarifications. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Louis VIII becomes King of France upon the death of his father, Philip II.
items.body: Louis VIII, nicknamed The Lion, was King of France from 1223 to 1226. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: A man ploughs a truck into a Bastille Day celebration in Nice, France, killing 86 people and injuring another 434 before being shot by police.
items.body: On the evening of 14 July 2016, a 19-tonne cargo truck was deliberately driven into crowds of people celebrating Bastille Day on the Promenade des Anglais in Nice, France, resulting in the deaths of 86 people and injuring 458 others. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: The Sedition Act of 1798 becomes law in the United States making it a federal crime to write, publish, or utter false or malicious statements about the United States government.
